title: frangula californica var. tomentella
intro: Frangula californica var. tomentella, also known as the California buckthorn, is a shrub-like plant native to the western United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its small, greenish-yellow flowers and grayish-brown bark. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its laxative properties, which are attributed to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides, particularly cascara and aloe-emodin. The health benefits of Frangula californica var. tomentella include relief from constipation, improved digestion, and possibly even anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Frangula californica var. tomentella is often prepared as a dried root, bark, or leaf extract, or as a tea, and can also be found in capsule or tincture form in some herbal supplements.
